Use this skill when building or reviewing backend services.

## Concepts (timeless)
- Layer the code: transport/handler → service (business logic) → repository (data access). Keep business logic independent of storage and framework.
- Validate untrusted input at the system boundary; never trust data from the wire.
- Make external effects idempotent and bounded (timeouts, retries with backoff, circuit-breaking).
- Return a consistent result/error shape; log detailed context server-side, surface safe messages outward.
- Prefer dependency injection over globals so the service is testable.

## Pull current docs first
Frameworks, ORMs, and client SDKs evolve — confirm the current API before coding:
- your framework's current docs (use Context7 or WebFetch)

## Checklist
- [ ] Clear handler/service/repository boundaries; no business logic in handlers
- [ ] Inputs validated at the boundary with a schema
- [ ] Timeouts + bounded retries on every outbound call
- [ ] No N+1 queries; queries are bounded/paginated
- [ ] Errors logged with context; no sensitive data leaked to clients
